item.title: Micron posts 15-fold profit surge in boost for global AI stocks
item.description: Chipmaker forecasts sustained demand for computer memory, boosting its shares and Asian markets

item.title: VW’s Everllence sale shows even ailing carmakers have hidden charms
item.description: Deal suggests carmakers may have more value packed away in the trunk

item.title: Electricity prices soar as Europe battles heatwave
item.description: Record June temperatures are fuelling demand for air conditioning and putting pressure on energy systems

item.title: Tesco’s return to broadband should be a profitable side hustle
item.description: Retailer has a good chance of snagging market share thanks to its brand recognition and captive customer base

item.title: TotalEnergies to account for role in greenhouse gas emissions, court rules
item.description: Partial victory for campaigners and city of Paris in attempt to attribute corporate responsibility for global warming

item.title: Competition intensifies for Anthropic and OpenAI ahead of IPOs
item.description: Renewed challenge from open-source models raises stakes on AI labs to make their case

item.title: Oil price back at prewar levels as Gulf flows pick up
item.description: Brent crude drops below the $72.48 a barrel it traded at in late February before Iran conflict erupted

item.title: Micron shares jump as blockbuster results spark Wall Street tech shake-up
item.description: Apple’s stock slides after company unveils price rises linked to ‘unprecedented’ cost of memory chips

item.title: US high court hands Bayer crucial victory over Roundup weedkiller
item.description: Justices overturn verdict in case alleging manufacturer failed to warn on herbicide’s alleged cancer risks

item.title: Iran tells ships in Strait of Hormuz to turn back
item.description: At least four vessels change course as Tehran challenges International Maritime Organization’s evacuation route

item.title: Vale shareholders head to vote after board rejects push to oust chair
item.description: Corporate governance battle at world’s largest iron ore miner revives fears of political meddling

item.title: Iraq pushes Opec to let it pump more oil
item.description: Baghdad’s crude sales were drastically reduced when US-Iran war blocked exports via Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Ryanair scraps family seating fees after regulator launches probe
item.description: Budget carrier says families can now opt for random allocation of free seats beside each other

item.title: UK bank to pay £32mn to clients of WealthTek
item.description: CACEIS UK, the sub-custodian bank for the collapsed wealth manager, comes to agreement with FCA

item.title: UK housebuilders warn of ‘shockwaves’ as tariffs on steel imports double
item.description: Construction industry says levies will impose punitive costs despite government’s effort to soften blow

item.title: Untethered Goose Game
item.description: Just SoftBank doing SoftBank things

item.title: The ever weirder world of venture capital
item.description: Why the Power Law remains a guiding principle of the high-risk, high-reward VC game

item.title: Anthropic has hired an economist with . . . interesting views on human survival
item.description: Skynet versus unending rainbows

item.title: Jamie Dimon promotes two potential successors at JPMorgan
item.description: Marianne Lake leaves bank as Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh handed two of its largest divisions

item.title: Apple increases MacBook and iPad prices by 20%
item.description: iPhone maker blamed cost rises on memory chip shortages caused by AI boom

item.title: France warns against weaker climate targets for cars as EV sales surge
item.description: Minister says watering down European engine rules would send ‘terrible signal’ after Hormuz energy crisis

item.title: Flagship Ares private credit fund hit by 14% withdrawal requests
item.description: Firm caps redemptions as retail investor exodus accelerates across the industry

item.title: Hospitals cancel operations because of lack of air conditioning
item.description: Record June temperatures put up to 4,000 surgeries at risk

item.title: Why aren’t more companies adopting AI?
item.description: Organisational barriers may still be too high

item.title: SpaceX bond sale signals markets are in ‘bubble territory’, warns Allianz CIO
item.description: Debt investors will scrutinise Musk’s rocket company more closely than equity markets, says Ludovic Subran

item.title: Donald Trump — EV salesman extraordinaire
item.description: Electric cars go vrooom

item.title: Robots are coming to the oil patch
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter, Russia receives an oil windfall amid Iran war

item.title: Market gloom reduces number of homes facing ‘mansion tax’ in England
item.description: Receipts would be nearly £30mn lower today than when the policy was announced, estate agency data shows

item.title: German drugmaker Merck to buy Bio-Techne in $11.3bn deal
item.description: Acquisition of US medical tools maker will be German group’s biggest in over a decade

item.title: How much compute does the world really need?
item.description: Scale cannot solve AI’s fundamental problem with accuracy
